name: Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Active Dry Yeast (or 2 tbsp Active Dry Yeast, Bulk)
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk in a saucepan.
2. Pour over the shortening, sugar, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
3. Cool to lukewarm.
4. Sprinkle the yeast over the lukewarm water and stir to dissolve.
5. Add the yeast mixture, 3 cups of flour, and eggs to the milk mixture.
6. Beat for about 2 minutes using an electric mixer set on medium speed.
7. Gradually stir in enough of the remaining flour to make a soft dough.
8. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth and satiny (about 8 to 10 minutes).
9. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning once to grease the top and cover.
10. Let rise in a warm place until doubled (about 1 hour).
11. Punch the dough down and divide into thirds.
12. Let the dough rest for 10 minutes.
13. Divide each third into 12 pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
14. Place 12 balls, equally spaced, in a greased 9-inch round cake pan. Repeat with the remaining two-thirds.
15. Cover and let rise until doubled (45 to 60 minutes).
16. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown.
17. Remove from pans and cool on wire racks.
